title: Define DTD
text: Define DTD Load, create, or edit Uniface-compliant Document Type Definitions (DTDs) for XML data, and map them to Uniface entities and fields. The form displays the DTD in its native text format. Properties Description Definition How to Working with DTDs Manually Defining DTDs Menu Options The following DTD options are available on the File menu: New DTD Open Save Duplicate DTD Delete DTD Load Component Structure —generates a DTD based on a component data structure Load from File —loads a DTD from the file system Save to File —saves the DTD as a file using the DTD format specified in the XML standard. It does not store your changes in the Repository, nor copy Uniface-specific information about the DTD to the file. For example, additional properties and mapping defined for the DTD are not included in the file. Validate —validates the DTD. If it does not validate, view the error messages in the Message Frame (Ctrl+M). Compile DTD Related Links Uniface-Compliant DTDs A Document Type Definition (DTD) defines rules for the structure of an XML stream. In Uniface, a DTD is a development object that includes DTD definitions and a default mapping between the XML elements defined by the DTD and Uniface component fields and entities. It can be used to define the rules for the structure of an XML stream that is exchanged between components. Examples of DTDs and XML Streams Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
